title: Lesson Quiz: Values and Work Preferences
description: Assessment of the concept that Work environments, lifestyle goals, and priorities.

questions.prompt: What is a value in career planning?

choices.text: A belief or priority that matters when making decisions
choices.feedback: Correct. Values are the priorities or beliefs that help guide decisions.

choices.text: A list of jobs in a career cluster
choices.feedback: A career cluster is different from a personal value.
A value is personal and helps shape decisions, not a school rule.
A contract is not the same as a value.

questions.prompt: Work preferences can include whether a student wants a structured schedule or flexible hours.

choices.feedback: Work preferences can include schedules, environments, and how a person likes to work.
Correct. Schedule and environment are common work preferences.

questions.prompt: Which example best shows a work preference?

choices.text: Memorizing vocabulary words for a quiz
choices.feedback: This is a study task, not a work preference.

choices.text: Joining a school club for one afternoon
choices.feedback: This does not define a long-term work preference.

choices.text: Wanting to work with people in a fast-paced environment
choices.feedback: Correct. This describes the kind of work setting and pace a student prefers.

choices.text: Choosing a locker near a classroom
choices.feedback: A locker choice is not a work preference.

questions.prompt: Why do values and work preferences matter in career exploration?

choices.text: They remove the need to learn about career pathways
choices.feedback: Students still need to explore pathways and job options.

choices.text: They guarantee one perfect career for every student
choices.feedback: Career exploration involves reflection and comparison, not guaranteed perfection.

choices.text: They matter only after a student writes a resume
choices.feedback: Values and work preferences matter early in career exploration.

choices.text: They help explain what kind of work and lifestyle may be a good fit
choices.feedback: Correct. Values and preferences help students compare careers more carefully.

questions.prompt: A student should choose a career based only on salary and ignore personal values.

choices.feedback: Correct. Career fit should consider values, priorities, and work preferences along with other factors.
Salary may matter, but students should also consider values and work preferences.
